title: Archetype Definition v2.0
description: Schema for defining image archetypes with target DNA v2.0 characteristics including 12-sector hue analysis.

engine.description: Quantization strategy. 'direct' = standard MK 1.5 posterization (default for all named archetypes). 'distilled' = over-quantize to 3×K then furthest-point sample back to K (used only by the Distilled pseudo-archetype for max tonal diversity at the cost of performance). User can always override.

preferred_sectors.description: Optional: Hue sectors this archetype favors (e.g., ['blue', 'cyan'] for Blue Rescue)

centroid.description: Target DNA values defining the archetype's position in 7D feature space

primary_sector_weight.description: Target weight of the dominant 30° sector

shadowClamp.description: Forces ink density between 0-N% up to exactly N%. Eliminates unprintable tints.

chromaGate.description: Multiplier for cWeight when DNA.maxC is high. Improves high-chroma color accuracy.

detailRescue.description: Reduces entropy noise floor by N points. Preserves small structural details.

speckleRescue.description: Morphological despeckle: removes isolated clusters below N pixels. Eliminates unprintable digital lint. Standard: 4 pixels for 230-mesh screens.

medianPass.description: Pre-posterization median filter (3×3): removes salt & pepper noise before palette generation. Essential for organic/sensor photos to prevent noise from becoming distinct colors. Disable for intentional halftone patterns.

minVolume.description: Palette post-pruning: merge colors with pixel coverage below N%. Resource allocation - weak colors (<minVolume) are merged to nearest neighbors. Prevents outlier/noise colors from claiming screens.

ghostFloor.description: Distilled-engine only. Minimum coverage fraction (0–0.1) for a color to enter FPS selection. Colors in the over-palette below this threshold are excluded before furthest-point sampling runs. Default 0.001 (0.1%). Raise to 0.01 (1%) for single-dominant-hue archetypes where FPS selects spectral outliers (stems, shadows) that have low coverage but high spectral distance from the dominant hue mass.

refinementPasses.description: K-means refinement passes after median cut. 0=skip (graphic/grayscale archetypes), 1=default single pass, 2+=extra convergence for complex photographic content.

chromaAxisWeight.description: Weight for C* (chroma magnitude) as a virtual 4th split axis in median cut. 0=disabled (default). When >0, boxes can split along the chroma gradient (e.g. C=5→C=145) independent of hue angle. Critical for warm images with smooth chroma ramps where Cartesian a/b splits miss the chroma dimension.

neutralIsolationThreshold.description: Pre-isolate neutral pixels (C* < threshold) into a separate box before median cut begins. 0=disabled (default). When >0, prevents the neutral majority from consuming split budget and dragging centroids toward low chroma. Particularly effective in images with >50% neutral content.
